element_introduction_order.mastery_context: resource management becomes critical in later dungeons

element_introduction_order.element: Screen-wide passages and rooms (X-Y coordinate grid)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: dual-purpose resource: ammunition for secondary weapons and shop currency
tactical combat options using power chips

element_introduction_order.element: Warp panels with symbol matching system

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: checkpoints, advice givers, password providers, upgrade traders
required for interior exploration and corridor navigation
primary objectives; activation sequence needed to win
require clues from Labyrinth rooms to unseal

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to Labyrinth after dungeon/boss completion
typical_tension_triggers: Discovering new sealed corridors with unknown activation methods
Multiple hostile alien tribes in territory
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-mode pacing: deliberate non-linear exploration in Labyrinth contrasted with intense linear progression and combat through enemy-filled shoot-em-up dungeons; tension escalates as player acquires keys, unlocks new regions, and must face increasingly dangerous corridors

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Sealed corridors with clue-based activation requirements; warp panel keys gate access to regions; multiple trophy routes to same objective
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Messages left by predecessors in environment; clues in rooms indicate how to unseal hidden corridors

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics through Labyrinth exploration; primary weapon sufficient for basic encounters; resource scarcity management begins
difficulty_curve.mid_game: First dungeon encounters introduce spaceship form and boss combat; secondary weapon tactical use required; key collection gates progression into new Labyrinth regions; upgrade acquisition becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple safety devices require corridor completion; sealed corridor puzzles demand clue-gathering and backtracking; boss difficulty escalates; resource management (power chips for ammo vs. upgrades) becomes strategic decision-making
notable_spikes: First dungeon entry (introduction to shoot-em-up mechanic and boss encounters)
Early sealed corridor discovery (introduces puzzle-gating mechanic)
Mid-game accumulation of safety device corridors (multiple simultaneous objectives)
Late-game boss difficulty scaling as alien faction territories grow more hostile

notable_level_patterns.description: Screen-wide rooms on X-Y coordinate grid; player navigates horizontally/vertically between passages; warp panels provide symbol-locked shortcuts; contains clues, enemy encounters, shops, or Blue Lander checkpoints; optional exploration branches

Enemy-filled corridor in spaceship form; vertical or horizontal shoot-em-up scrolling; player progresses toward boss; defeat boss to deactivate safety device and earn power-up reward; return to Labyrinth on completion

Corridor marked as sealed/inactive in Labyrinth; requires specific action or item from environment to unlock; completion yields safety device deactivation or region key; encourages backtracking and clue-gathering
Safe encounter space where Blue Landers trade power chips for upgrades, provide passwords, or serve as checkpoints; no combat; allows strategic planning and resource conversion
